clusters.title: Trump heads to China for state visit. And, how the war in Iran has affected inflation
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
bullet_summary: President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage
Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage

items.title: Trump heads to China for state visit. And, how the war in Iran has affected inflation
items.standfirst: Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.
items.content: Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)

items.title: Trump heads to China today for high-stakes meeting with Xi
items.standfirst: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
items.content: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

items.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
items.standfirst: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran to make a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.
items.content: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran to make a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.

items.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
items.standfirst: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".
items.content: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

items.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran on ‘life support’ after rejecting peace proposals
items.standfirst: US president says he is considering restarting naval escorts in strait of Hormuz in attempt to end Iranian blockadeDonald Trump has said the ceasefire with Iran is on “life support” and that he is considering restarting US navy military escorts of ships through the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to end the Iranian blockade of the vital waterway.The US president dismissed Iran’s peace proposals as stupid, and denied he was under any domestic pressure to reach a deal. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says he is considering restarting naval escorts in strait of Hormuz in attempt to end Iranian blockadeDonald Trump has said the ceasefire with Iran is on “life support” and that he is considering restarting US navy military escorts of ships through the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to end the Iranian blockade of the vital waterway.The US president dismissed Iran’s peace proposals as stupid, and denied he was under any domestic pressure to reach a deal. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says ceasefire is ‘on life support’ after rejecting ‘unacceptable’ proposal from Iran
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage.”
items.content: President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is “on life support” and “unbelievably weak” after the two sides exchanged fire this weekend, with Trump labeling Iran’s peace proposal as “garbage.”

items.title: Oil prices climb after Trump dismisses Iran’s response to peace plan
items.standfirst: Brent crude rises after US president calls overture from Tehran ‘totally unacceptable’Oil prices have climbed after Donald Trump condemned Iran’s response to US proposals to end the war as “totally unacceptable”.The president’s rejection of Tehran’s overture in a post on his Truth Social platform triggered a jump in Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil prices, by as much as 4% on Monday to $105.50 a barrel, before easing back to settle at $103.50. Continue reading...
items.content: Brent crude rises after US president calls overture from Tehran ‘totally unacceptable’Oil prices have climbed after Donald Trump condemned Iran’s response to US proposals to end the war as “totally unacceptable”.The president’s rejection of Tehran’s overture in a post on his Truth Social platform triggered a jump in Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil prices, by as much as 4% on Monday to $105.50 a barrel, before easing back to settle at $103.50. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
items.standfirst: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.
items.content: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

items.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon.
items.content: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon.

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, CBS News, Financial Times and others

items.title: US inflation jumps to 3.8% as energy costs surge from Iran war
items.standfirst: The key measure of US inflation rose in April to its highest level since May 2023 as consumers feel the impact of the Iran war.
items.content: The key measure of US inflation rose in April to its highest level since May 2023 as consumers feel the impact of the Iran war.

items.title: CPI surged in April as inflation soars to highest level in almost 3 years
items.standfirst: U.S. consumer prices rose in April, fueled by a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran war.
items.content: U.S. consumer prices rose in April, fueled by a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran war.

items.title: US inflation jumps to 3.8% as Middle East war stokes price rises
items.standfirst: April figure marks highest level in three years as effects of conflict reverberate through US economy
items.content: April figure marks highest level in three years as effects of conflict reverberate through US economy

items.title: Inflation jumps to its highest level since 2023. Here are 3 things costing a lot more
items.standfirst: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.
items.content: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago. (Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)

items.title: Inflation surged to 3.8% in April, its highest level in nearly three years
items.standfirst: Inflation surged to 3.8% in April, its highest level in nearly three years, as the war in Iran causes a ripple effect across the economy and energy prices rise.
items.content: Inflation surged to 3.8% in April, its highest level in nearly three years, as the war in Iran causes a ripple effect across the economy and energy prices rise.

clusters.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
clusters.neutral_headline: US passengers of hantavirus cruise ship being monitored in Nebraska
bullet_summary: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship
All passengers of the cruiseship hit by hantavirus have all evacuated, with several cases confirmed
Three cruiseship passengers have died and at least seven people with confirmed cases of the virus are among those being quarantined, including a French woman

items.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
items.standfirst: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.
items.content: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

items.title: Could the hantavirus bring back Covidlike safety precautions?
items.standfirst: All passengers of the cruiseship hit by hantavirus have all evacuated, with several cases confirmed. According to the World Health Organisation, there is nothing to far as this is not another Covid. But some still fear it could trigger the return of safety precautions for the wider public - including mandatory mask wearing. But is that really likely?
items.content: All passengers of the cruiseship hit by hantavirus have all evacuated, with several cases confirmed. According to the World Health Organisation, there is nothing to far as this is not another Covid. But some still fear it could trigger the return of safety precautions for the wider public - including mandatory mask wearing. But is that really likely?

items.title: MV Hondius passengers all evacuated with at least seven confirmed cases
items.standfirst: All of the passengers have now been evacuated from a cruiseship hit by an outbreak of the hantavirus. The MV Hondius had been docked in the Spanish Island of Tenerife; it will now head to the Netherlands. Three cruiseship passengers have died and at least seven people with confirmed cases of the virus are among those being quarantined, including a French woman.
items.content: All of the passengers have now been evacuated from a cruiseship hit by an outbreak of the hantavirus. The MV Hondius had been docked in the Spanish Island of Tenerife; it will now head to the Netherlands. Three cruiseship passengers have died and at least seven people with confirmed cases of the virus are among those being quarantined, including a French woman.

items.title: US passengers of hantavirus cruise ship being monitored in Nebraska
items.standfirst: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, 16 in Nebraska and another six passengers in Canada.
items.content: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, 16 in Nebraska and another six passengers in Canada.

items.title: Why Americans from the hantavirus-stricken ship were taken to Omaha
items.standfirst: Most of the Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak were taken to specialized facilities at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.
items.content: Most of the Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak were taken to specialized facilities at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.

items.title: Hantavirus in the U.S.: How easily does the Andes strain spread?
items.standfirst: At least 11 passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship have tested positive for the Andes strain of hantavirus.
items.content: At least 11 passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship have tested positive for the Andes strain of hantavirus.

items.title: Americans from hantavirus-hit cruise ship being monitored for observation, U.S. officials say
items.standfirst: The 18 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius arrived back in the U.S. on Monday, and two of them are in biocontainment units in Omaha, Nebraska, and Atlanta
items.content: The 18 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius arrived back in the U.S. on Monday, and two of them are in biocontainment units in Omaha, Nebraska, and Atlanta

clusters.title: WHO head tells countries to prepare for more hantavirus cases
clusters.neutral_headline: WHO head tells countries to prepare for more hantavirus cases
bullet_summary: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, BBC World

items.title: WHO head tells countries to prepare for more hantavirus cases
items.standfirst: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus praises Spain’s ‘compassion and solidarity’ in evacuating virus-hit cruise shipThe head of the World Health Organization has told countries to prepare for more hantavirus cases after the outbreak onboard the MV Hondius, and thanked Spain for the “compassion and solidarity” it had shown by taking in the stricken cruise ship and evacuating its passengers and crew.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus urged authorities to follow the WHO’s advice and recommendations, which include a 42-day quarantine and constant monitoring of high-risk contacts. Continue reading...
items.content: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus praises Spain’s ‘compassion and solidarity’ in evacuating virus-hit cruise shipThe head of the World Health Organization has told countries to prepare for more hantavirus cases after the outbreak onboard the MV Hondius, and thanked Spain for the “compassion and solidarity” it had shown by taking in the stricken cruise ship and evacuating its passengers and crew.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus urged authorities to follow the WHO’s advice and recommendations, which include a 42-day quarantine and constant monitoring of high-risk contacts. Continue reading...

items.title: WHO chief says "work not over" after evacuation of hantavirus-stricken ship
items.standfirst: The head of the World Health Organization says "our work is not over" to contain hantavirus after evacuations from a cruise ship​ hit by a deadly outbreak of the illness.
items.content: The head of the World Health Organization says "our work is not over" to contain hantavirus after evacuations from a cruise ship​ hit by a deadly outbreak of the illness.

items.title: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
items.standfirst: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.
items.content: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

clusters.title: Deaths of at least six people believed to be due to heat inside shipping container in Texas
clusters.neutral_headline: Six people found dead inside train car at rail yard near Texas-Mexico border
bullet_summary: Officials reportedly have also said the death of a seventh person whose body was found near railroad tracks outside San Antonio, Texas – 150 miles (241km) to the north – may be connected to the case
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Deaths of at least six people believed to be due to heat inside shipping container in Texas
items.standfirst: Seventh body found near railroad tracks thought to be connected to what authorities call ‘potential human smuggling event’Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailFederal agents are investigating the deaths of at least six people thought to be immigrants found inside a shipping container at a Union Pacific rail yard near the border with Mexico in Laredo, Texas, on Sunday as a “potential human smuggling event”.Officials reportedly have also said the death of a seventh person whose body was found near railroad tracks outside San Antonio, Texas – 150 miles (241km) to the north – may be connected to the case.Guardian staff contributed reporting Continue reading...
items.content: Seventh body found near railroad tracks thought to be connected to what authorities call ‘potential human smuggling event’Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailFederal agents are investigating the deaths of at least six people thought to be immigrants found inside a shipping container at a Union Pacific rail yard near the border with Mexico in Laredo, Texas, on Sunday as a “potential human smuggling event”.Officials reportedly have also said the death of a seventh person whose body was found near railroad tracks outside San Antonio, Texas – 150 miles (241km) to the north – may be connected to the case.Guardian staff contributed reporting Continue reading...

items.title: Texas sheriff says 7th body could be tied to shipping container deaths
items.standfirst: The body of a seventh person was located Monday nearly 150 miles north of a Union Pacific rail yard in Laredo, where six bodies were discovered on Sunday afternoon.
items.content: The body of a seventh person was located Monday nearly 150 miles north of a Union Pacific rail yard in Laredo, where six bodies were discovered on Sunday afternoon.

items.title: Six people found dead inside train car at rail yard near Texas-Mexico border
items.standfirst: Discovery was made by Union Pacific employee inspecting stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey northSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailRail workers in Texas found six people dead inside a boxcar at a yard close to the Mexican border on Sunday afternoon, officials said.The discovery was made by a Union Pacific employee inspecting the stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey north, a spokesperson for the Laredo police department said, citing the railroad freight company. Continue reading...
items.content: Discovery was made by Union Pacific employee inspecting stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey northSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailRail workers in Texas found six people dead inside a boxcar at a yard close to the Mexican border on Sunday afternoon, officials said.The discovery was made by a Union Pacific employee inspecting the stopped train at the yard in Laredo before it continued its journey north, a spokesperson for the Laredo police department said, citing the railroad freight company. items.standfirst: Instructure Inc. said it reached an agreement with hackers who disrupted universities’ online coursework and testing on the company’s Canvas platform, with the attackers returning stolen data and pledging not to extort the education software maker’s customers.
items.content: Instructure Inc. said it reached an agreement with hackers who disrupted universities’ online coursework and testing on the company’s Canvas platform, with the attackers returning stolen data and pledging not to extort the education software maker’s customers.

items.content: The company behind Canvas says it has "reached an agreement" with the hackers who disrupted thousands of colleges and universities.

clusters.title: European Union adopts new sanctions Israeli settlers over violence in West Bank
clusters.neutral_headline: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
bullet_summary: The EU's foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences", while Israel branded the move "arbitrary"
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: European Union adopts new sanctions Israeli settlers over violence in West Bank
items.standfirst: European Union foreign ministers on May 12 agreed new sanctions on Israeli settlers over violence against Palestinians, as a change of government in Hungary ended months of blockage. The bloc also moved to sanction leaders from the Palestinian militant group Hamas. Israel has condemned the sanctions -- calling them baseless.
items.content: European Union foreign ministers on May 12 agreed new sanctions on Israeli settlers over violence against Palestinians, as a change of government in Hungary ended months of blockage. The bloc also moved to sanction leaders from the Palestinian militant group Hamas. Israel has condemned the sanctions -- calling them baseless.

items.content: The EU's foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences", while Israel branded the move "arbitrary".

clusters.title: 'There is a big rejection of Starmer' from Labour voters as PM faces calls to resign
clusters.neutral_headline: UK prime minister faces growing internal pressure to resign
bullet_summary: British Prime Minister Kier Starmer is under mounting pressure to step down a day after he said his departure would bring instability
He's been facing the heat since last week's dismal election results showed the Labour Party lost significant ground nearly two years after sweeping to victory in the General Election

items.title: 'There is a big rejection of Starmer' from Labour voters as PM faces calls to resign
items.standfirst: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer told his top team that he was getting on with governing, defying mounting calls from ministers and MPs to step down. This comes following the resignation of a junior minister, who called again for his departure. But for Starmer and his supporters, stepping down would bring in a new era of political turmoil. The situation may seem at odds with Labour's landslide victory two years ago. But for FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley, there are several reasons that explain it.
items.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer told his top team that he was getting on with governing, defying mounting calls from ministers and MPs to step down. This comes following the resignation of a junior minister, who called again for his departure. But for Starmer and his supporters, stepping down would bring in a new era of political turmoil. The situation may seem at odds with Labour's landslide victory two years ago. But for FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley, there are several reasons that explain it.

items.title: UK prime minister faces growing internal pressure to resign
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Kier Starmer is under mounting pressure to step down a day after he said his departure would bring instability. He's been facing the heat since last week's dismal election results showed the Labour Party lost significant ground nearly two years after sweeping to victory in the General Election. Among those asking him to resign are several of his cabinet ministers.
items.content: British Prime Minister Kier Starmer is under mounting pressure to step down a day after he said his departure would bring instability. He's been facing the heat since last week's dismal election results showed the Labour Party lost significant ground nearly two years after sweeping to victory in the General Election. Among those asking him to resign are several of his cabinet ministers.

items.title: LIVE: Starmer under pressure as ministers quit, 80 MPs urge him to resign
items.standfirst: The British prime minister is facing a crisis after his Labour Party suffered election losses.
items.content: The British prime minister is facing a crisis after his Labour Party suffered election losses.
